data.title: Crypto giant Binance launches in Syria after U.S. sanctions lift
data.description:
Crypto exchange Binance Holdings said Thursday it is expanding operations to Syria, enabling local users to access its platform for the first time as U.S. sanctions are rolling back.

In mid-May, U.S. President Donald Trump said he will order an end to the sanctions on Syria to give the country's new government "a chance of peace."

"Now that sanctions have been suspended, the Syrian community can finally act on that [crypto] interest," Binance said in a statement.

Now, Syrian residents can use Binance to access its crypto-focused products and services, from spot and futures trading to cross border remittances.

As recently as 2021, Syria ranked among the top 10 countries globally crypto crypto-related search activity, the company noted.

data.title: Stocks open lower as geopolitical, trade tensions weigh; bonds rally on soft PPI
data.description:
U.S. stocks opened lower on Thursday, as sentiment was weighed down by geopolitical risks and trade tensions. However, bonds rallied on a benign producer inflation report.    

Minutes after the bell, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.3%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.6%.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported a +0.1% M/M rise in the producer price index (PPI) for May vs. +0.2% expected and -0.2% in April. The core PPI also rose +0.1% vs. +0.3% consensus and -0.1% in April. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved sharply lower after the PPI report, as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.37%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.90%.

Despite the soft PPI data, headlines on Thursday were dominated by rising geopolitical risks in the Middle East, a renewed tariff threat by U.S. President Donald Trump, and a crash of an Air India Boeing (BA) aircraft.    

Trump said he intended to send letters to trading partners in the next week or two setting unilateral tariff rates, ahead of a July 9 deadline to reimpose higher duties on dozens of countries.

"Trump said they would send letters to U.S. trading partners, detailing the threatened 9 July unilateral trade taxes. Market reactions have been a muted negative; investors have become conditioned to expect retreats from such threats. However, the uncertainty created may weigh on US investment and hiring in the near term," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Traders also continued to await any details on the U.S.-China trade deal. 

On geopolitical news, the U.S. government on Wednesday ordered the departure of embassy staff from Baghdad after Iran threatened to attack U.S. bases if talks over its nuclear program fall through.

"Sentiment wasn’t helped by various headlines, and CBS News reported that part of the reason for the U.S. moving staff out of the Middle East was because U.S. officials had been told that Israel was ready to launch an operation into Iran," Reid said.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

data.title: Conagra faces meaningful headwinds from protein inflation, demand elasticities - BofA Securities
data.description:
Facing cost pressures for chicken, beef and pork exacerbated by limited pricing power, Conagra (NYSE:CAG) faces a “meaningful” headwind to earnings growth along with increased margin pressures, leading BofA Securities to downgrade the packaged food producer to Underperform from Neutral, and shave 26% off its price target.

“Conagra faces unique challenges within packaged food heading into FY26,” said BofA’s Peter Galbo, as the company’s largest category – single-serve frozen meals— already has elevated demand elasticities.

The company recently debuted more than 50 new frozen foods options, including gluten-free and plant-based meals, and sold its Chef Boyardee, Mrs. Pauls’ and Van de Kamp’s brands. Although necessary, Galbo believes these actions will also likely be a drag on earnings over the next year, especially in consideration of decelerating sales in its frozen foods category and its exposure to elasticities from protein-cost relating pricing.

For the current fiscal year, the company estimated COGS inflation at ~4% which is offset by an underlying ~4% COGS productivity. But in FY26, Galbo estimates an unchanged COGS productivity of ~4% to be more than offset by ~6% COGS inflation. And given Conagra’s (NYSE:CAG) limited pricing price, FY26 gross margin could compress by as much as 100-150 basis points from FY25.

“Our Underperform rating is predicated on our view that earnings will be meaningfully pressured by reacceleration of inflation for COGS basket in FY26 without significant pricing power…in addition CAG’s FY26 leverage targets and dividend payout ratio appears to be at risk,” Galbo says in Thursday’s note to investors.

Galbo’s current $20 price target suggests 10% downside to Wednesday’s closing price.

Conagra (NYSE:CAG) shares opened 2% lower, setting a new 6 ½ year low.

data.title: Egypt to buy LNG from Shell, Saudi Aramco and others - Reuters
data.description:
Egypt has reached agreements with several energy firms and trading houses to buy 150-160 cargoes of liquefied natural gas costing more than $8B to cover power demand through the end of 2026, Reuters reported Thursday, citing industry sources aware of the matter.

Agreements reportedly have been reached with global energy companies and trading houses including Shell (NYSE:SHEL), Saudi Aramco (ARMCO), Vitol, Trafigura, BGN, SOCAR and PetroChina.

The move to secure cargoes is aimed at cutting Egypt's reliance on volatile spot markets, but also emphasizes a sharp turnaround in a country that until just a year ago was exporting LNG.

Egypt also is tying to boost its economy after emerging from a foreign currency crisis, and a shift to medium-term LNG deals leaves it less exposed to the volatility of the spot LNG market.

Egypt has endured rolling blackouts over the past two years as natural gas supply fell short of demand, and the country's own gas production in February hit its lowest level in nine years.

data.title: Most shorted S&P500 stocks in May
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) was the most shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in May, while Super Micro Computer (SMCI), which was at the top spot the in April, was at third position for the latest month.

Trump bill bearish for Enphase

Enphase Energy (ENPH) has plunged in the aftermath of President Donald Trump’s ‘one big beautiful bill’ that puts a stop to investments in and electricity production credits for clean energy facilities. The credits have been a key driver for utility-scale solar projects, leading investors worried for Enphase, a leading supplier of micro-inverter-based solar and battery systems.

Moderna: Challenges continue to mount

Moderna (MRNA) took the second spot, with a short interest representing 19.23% of the float, up from 17.58% the prior month. The biotechnology company has lost just over a third of its valuation in the year so far, weighed down by declining COVID-19 vaccine sales. In recent weeks, the company has withdrawn its application for a combined flu and Covid vaccine candidate and lost $700M in funding for late-stage development of a bird flu vaccine for humans.

Super Micro slips ranks, shares improve

Super Micro (SMCI) had 18.85% of its shares sold short as of end-May, down from 21.15% on April 30 and 22.84% at the end of March. It managed to slip down the ranks to the third-most shorted S&P 500 stock in the latest month, and the AI company’s shares made a 28% recovery in the last 30 days. Shares are also up 41.8% YTD.

Here are the top 20 most shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of May: 

  • Enphase Energy (ENPH) - Short Interest as a % of float: 19.27%
  • Moderna (MRNA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 19.23%
  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) - Short Interest as a % of float: 18.85%
  • Albemarle (ALB) - Short Interest as a % of float: 15.92%
  • Omnicom Group Inc. (OMC) - Short Interest as a % of float: 13.48%
  • Paramount Global (PARA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 13.48%
  • Caesars Entertainment (CZR) - Short Interest as a % of float: 12.82%
  • Charter Communications (CHTR) - Short Interest as a % of float: 12.21%
  • Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.88%
  • APA (APA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.72%
  • First Solar (FSLR) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.58%
  • MGM Resorts International (MGM) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.27%
  • Brown-Forman (BF.B) - Short Interest as a % of float: 9.78%
  • The Campbell's Company (CPB) - Short Interest as a % of float: 9.72%
  • Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) - Short Interest as a % of float: 9.41%
  • Franklin Resources (BEN) - Short Interest as a % of float: 9.33%

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: BA, MU, ADBE and RH
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday were lower, as sentiment was weighed down by trade tensions and geopolitical risks. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday: 

  • Shares of Boeing (NYSE:BA) were -5.8% in pre-market trading. The decline came after one of the U.S. planemaker's 787 Dreamliners crashed in the western city of Ahmedabad in India on Thursday. The London-bound Air India flight went down minutes after taking off, crashing into a residential neighborhood while carrying 242 people on board. The crash raised fresh questions about the safety of Boeing's (NYSE:BA) planes. 
  • Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) stock was in focus, -1% ahead of the opening bell. The memory chipmaker said it would spend about $200B on U.S. semiconductor manufacturing and research and development, in another example of a big company pledging domestic investments following U.S. President Donald Trump's return. Micron (NASDAQ:MU) said the new investment will create an estimated 90,000 direct and indirect jobs.
  • Shares of Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) will garner attention, ahead of the creative software maker's FQ2 2025 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the San Jose, Calif.-based firm to earn $4.97 per share on revenue of $5.80B. Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) is known for its Photoshop graphics editor and Premier Pro video editor, but investors will be looking at the performance of the company's generative AI offerings. 
  • RH (NYSE:RH) stock will get some eyes, ahead of the Corte Madera, Calif.-based firm's Q1 earnings after hours. Wall Street expects the upscale home-furnishings retailer to narrow its quarterly loss to 7 cents per share on revenue of $818.57M. Still, overall demand has been subdued due to lower consumer discretionary spending. Keybanc last month raised concerns after RH (NYSE:RH) boosted member discounts.

data.title: Hycroft Mining prices upsized public offering of units; shares down 25%
data.description:
• Hycroft Mining (NASDAQ:HYMC) is offering 12.5M units at $3.50/unit.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one-half warrant exercisable at $4.20/share for 36 months.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to total ~$43.75M before expenses.
  • An additional 1.875M units may be sold under a 30-day underwriter option.
  • The offering is expected to close on June 13, 2025.
  • Proceeds will fund exploration, working capital, and general corporate purposes.
  • HYMC shares fall over 25% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Glucotrack announces 1-for-60 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Glucotrack (NASDAQ:GCTK) on Thursday said it will affect a 1-for-60 reverse stock split of its issued and outstanding common stock par value $0.001 per share, effective with the opening of trading on Monday, June 16, 2025.
  • Glucotrack’s common stock will continue to trade on the Nasdaq under the symbol “GCTK”.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to bring Glucotrack into compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for maintaining the listing on Nasdaq.
  • GCTK -25% premarket to $0.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China Yuchai declares $0.53 dividend
data.description:
• China Yuchai (NYSE:CYD) declares $0.53/share annual dividend.
  • Forward yield 2.77%
  • Payable July 7; for shareholders of record June 25; ex-div June 25.
  • See CYD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: DoubleDown Interactive announces pricing of secondary share sale
data.description:
• DoubleDown Interactive (NASDAQ:DDI) has announced the pricing of an underwritten secondary offering of 4,347,827 American Depositary Shares by STIC Special Situation Diamond.
  • The shares are being sold at a public offering price of $8.50 per ADS.
  • The seller has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 652,173 additional ADSs at the public offering price.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the ADSs by the seller. The offering is expected to close on or about June 13, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Shares are down 17% premarket.
